lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 -- At the Restaurant -- Would like and ordering food
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sandra Hayasaki | Aula 10 -- At the Restaurant

listenings.text: Hello, I am a waiter. I work at a small restaurant. Every day, I give the menu to the people. They look at the menu, and then they order. Many people say: I would like a coffee, please. Some people are very hungry, so they order a big plate of food. At the end, they ask for the bill. The food here is delicious, and the people are always happy.

Hi, my name is Anna. Today I am at a restaurant with my family. I am very hungry. The waiter gives us the menu. I would like a salad and some water, please. My brother would like a coffee and a dessert. The food is delicious. After we eat, my father asks for the bill. We are happy, and we say thank you to the waiter.

extra_audio.text: Let me tell you about Sandra at the restaurant. First, she reads the menu. Then, she calls the waiter. After that, she orders a salad and a coffee. Finally, she asks for the bill and pays.
